The Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA), 2005 guarantees 100 days of wage employment in a financial year to every rural household whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work.
Section 3 of MGNREGA: Every rural household shall have the right to apply for registration and the Gram Panchayat shall register the household after making due inquiry.

Job Card: Every registered household shall be issued a job card containing the details of the adult members of the household who are willing to do unskilled manual work. The job card shall be the property of the household.

Section 6 – Wages: The wage rate for work done under MGNREGA shall not be less than the wage rate notified by the State Government for the area. Wages shall be disbursed within 15 days of the work done.

Unemployment Allowance: If work is not provided within 15 days of the application, the applicant is entitled to unemployment allowance at the rate of one-fourth the wage rate for the first 30 days and one-half of the wage rate for the remaining period of the financial year.

Section 5 – Conditions of Employment: The Central Government shall notify minimum features to be included in the scheme, including provision of work site facilities such as crèche, drinking water, shade, and first aid.

Section 14 – Programme Officer: The Programme Officer shall be responsible for ensuring that applicants for work are given employment within 15 days of the date of application. If employment is not provided within 15 days, the Programme Officer shall process unemployment allowance claims.

Section 17 – Social Audit: The Gram Sabha shall conduct a social audit of all the projects under the scheme taken up in the Gram Panchayat. The Gram Panchayat shall make available all relevant documents for social audit including muster rolls, bills and vouchers.

Section 23 – Grievance Redressal: Any person or Gram Sabha may file a complaint with the Programme Officer or any higher authority about any violation of the provisions of the Act. The complaint shall be addressed within 7 days.

Schedule I – List of Permissible Works: Water conservation and water harvesting; Drought proofing including afforestation and tree plantation; Irrigation canals including micro and minor irrigation works; Provision of irrigation facility to land owned by SC/ST households; Land development; Flood control and protection works; Rural connectivity to provide all-weather access; Any other work that may be notified by the Central Government.

Section 24 – Transparency and Accountability: The Gram Panchayat shall maintain records of all works, employment provided, wages disbursed and social audits. These records shall be made available to the public on demand.

Women's Rights under MGNREGA: Not less than one-third of the beneficiaries, who have been issued job cards and have requested for employment under the Scheme shall be women who have registered under the Scheme.

Section 7 – Entitlement to Wages: Wages shall be paid directly to the bank or post office account of the worker. No payment shall be made in cash. The implementing agency shall ensure that wages are credited within 15 days.

Complaint Procedure: A worker can file a written complaint to the Programme Officer, District Programme Coordinator, or the State Government if wages are delayed, work is not provided, or the job card is not issued. The authority must respond within 7 days.

Section 28 – Role of Gram Panchayat: At least 50% of the works under the scheme shall be executed through the Gram Panchayat. The Gram Panchayat shall maintain muster rolls and ensure proper utilisation of funds.

Documents required for Job Card: An application form duly filled in, proof of residence (ration card, voter ID, etc.), proof of identity (Aadhaar card, voter ID, etc.), passport-sized photographs of all adult members.

Section 12 – District Programme Coordinator: The District Collector or the District Programme Coordinator shall supervise and monitor all the schemes under the Act, ensure timely payment of wages, and address complaints.

Material to Labour Ratio: The expenditure on materials including skilled labour shall not exceed 40% of the total expenditure on any project. At least 60% of the cost shall be on wages of unskilled manual workers.
Section 31 – Penalty for Non-compliance: Any person who fails to comply with or contravenes any provision of this Act shall be punishable with a fine which may extend to one thousand rupees.

Annual Report: The State Government shall prepare an annual report on the implementation of the scheme and submit it to the Central Government. The report shall include the number of job cards issued, employment provided, wages disbursed and social audits conducted.
